MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Plaintiff Empire Distribution, Inc.'s unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Brian D. Caplan Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Brian D. Caplan has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) If none, that must be so declared (as it was by counsel Grosz). The requirements are straightforward and set out clearly in the Rule of Court. Counsel should take care to fulfill them.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
